description:
GOAL: Wire heap-allocated tuples through the CPS pipeline.

## Decisions captured

- Tuple of arity N -> HeapKind::Struct{schema_id} where schema is N FzValue fields. Reuses the foundational struct mechanism (mostly free given .11.2).
- Schema registered per arity (1, 2, 3, ...) at compile-time. Could extend to per-distinct-shape later if profitable; per-arity simpler.
- IR prim: Prim::AllocStruct(schema_id, [f0, f1, ...]). Projection by static index becomes a field load.

## Deliverables

- IR codegen for Prim::AllocStruct (used for tuples; reused later for user records).
- Schema registration for arities encountered in the program.
- Tuple destructuring in pattern lowering (already covered in .11.4 — this connects).

## Tests

- `fn main() do print({1, 2}) end`.
- `fn fst({a, _}): a; fn snd({_, b}): b`.
- Tuple of mixed types: `{1, :ok, true}`.
- GC traces tuple fields (allocate tuple of pointers; drop outer; GC; verify pointed-to-objects freed).

## Out of scope

- User-definable struct syntax (future epic).
title: Tuples as struct schema (heap-allocated)
